title: Effects of bemisia tabaci and macrolophus pygmaeus on morphophysiological traits of plants
description: A study assessing the impact of the whitefly pest Bemisia tabaci alone or in combination with the predatory mirid bug Macrolophus pygmaeus on the morphological and physiological parameters of solanaceous vegetable crops, particularly tomato and eggplant. Results demonstrate species-specific crop susceptibility to B. tabaci and reveal that M. pygmaeus significantly mitigates the pest’s negative effects on plant growth and photosynthetic performance.
